answers.expected_answer: changelog.dto.ts defines changelog entry structure. ChangelogEntry is EnumCommentableEntity (supports comments). Changelog tracks: version releases, feature additions, bug fixes, breaking changes. EnumCommentType includes GENERAL and SYSTEM for changelog annotations. Used for: user communication, support reference, audit compliance. Changelog entries may link to: git commits, issue trackers, affected entities. Comments on changelog enable: user feedback, clarification requests, deployment notes.

Room management flow: 1) Socket.io rooms used for session-specific broadcasting, 2) On subscribeToSession: client.join(sessionId) adds socket to room, 3) subscribeToSession() also adds to internal sessionSubscriptions Map, 4) Broadcast: server.to(sessionId).emit('progressUpdate', data) sends to all room members, 5) On unsubscribe: client.leave(sessionId) removes from room, unsubscribeFromSession() updates Map, 6) On disconnect: iterate client.sessionIds, call unsubscribeFromSession for each, 7) Session completion cleanup: setTimeout after 30s, delete from sessionSubscriptions Map, 8) Room auto-cleans when empty (Socket.io behavior).

Next.js route groups (parentheses folders) don't affect URL structure but enable shared layouts. The (dashboard) group likely wraps authenticated pages with a common layout including sidebar, navbar, and auth checks. Parallel routes and layouts let dashboard/barrel-inventory, dashboard/jobs, dashboard/chat share navigation while each having unique content. Pages outside (dashboard) like clear-msal-cache are public utilities. This structure centralizes auth protection at the layout level.

SerialRegistryModule manages the assignment and tracking of serial number sequences. While barrels have serialNumber fields, the registry ensures uniqueness and may handle sequence generation (lot.sequence format like '25J03A.01.0001'). This separation allows batch serial allocation, prevents duplicates across concurrent operations, and tracks which ranges have been used. It's a critical service for barrel creation workflows ensuring no collision.

PrinterAutomationModule integrates with RabbitMQ for async print job delivery to physical printers. It has both a controller (API endpoints for triggering prints) and resolver (GraphQL mutations). The module uses BullModule for job queuing and imports CustomerLogoModule via forwardRef for label printing that includes customer branding. This architecture decouples print request handling from actual printing, supporting retries, status tracking, and multiple printer backends.

mapTransferTypeToGroupTransactionType is a utility function imported from './utils/transfer-type-mapper'. It converts: 'TRANSFER_IN' string -> EnumGroupTransactionType.TRANSFER_IN, 'TRANSFER_OUT' -> EnumGroupTransactionType.TRANSFER_OUT. Used in createLotLevelTransfer() to convert input transferType string to enum. The function likely also returns EnumFinancialDirection (IN or OUT) as part of TransferTypeMapping. This separation enables: type-safe conversion, centralized mapping logic, easier testing of conversion rules, handling of edge cases like 'OWNERSHIP_TRANSFER' type.

Frontend API routes in app/api/ serve two purposes: graphql-proxy/route.ts forwards authenticated GraphQL requests to the backend with token handling, while other routes (barrel-inventory/, ownership/, transfers/) make direct REST calls to backend endpoints. This hybrid approach uses GraphQL for complex queries needing selective field loading, and REST routes for simpler operations or file uploads. API routes also handle rate limiting and origin validation before forwarding.

The ContextService uses AsyncLocalStorage from Node.js to maintain request-scoped context across async operations. It stores userId, sessionId, correlationId, and request metadata. The ContextMiddleware extracts the user ID from req.user and runs the rest of the request handler within the context using contextService.run({ userId }, callback). This allows any service to access getCurrentUserId() or getContext() without explicitly passing user information through method parameters. The AuditService uses ContextService to automatically capture the current user for audit logs. The context is thread-safe and properly propagates through Promise chains and async/await patterns.

The BarrelModule imports BarrelModuleBase (base CRUD operations), AuthModule (authentication/authorization), BarrelOwnershipModule (ownership tracking), LotVariationModule (lot variation handling), AuditModule (audit logging), and ContextModule (request context). forwardRef is used for AuthModule and BarrelOwnershipModule to resolve circular dependencies - BarrelModule needs ownership services, but ownership may reference barrels back, creating a circular import that forwardRef lazily resolves.

Integration test strategy: 1) Test database: separate PostgreSQL instance or test schema, 2) Test Redis: separate instance or mock, 3) BullMQ processors: instantiate in test, process jobs synchronously, 4) GraphQL: use supertest to call endpoints with test auth tokens, 5) Mock Azure AD: stub validateTokenAndGetUser to return test user, 6) WebSocket tests: use Socket.io client to connect and verify events, 7) Data setup: seed test data via Prisma, clean up after tests, 8) Assertions: verify database state, event emissions, error handling, 9) Coverage: happy path, error scenarios, edge cases (empty batches, duplicates), 10) CI integration: run in isolated environment with test configs.

Resolution services provide serial number suggestions for error correction: predict-next-9xxx-serial.dto.ts handles 9xxx-series temporary serials (used when actual serial unclear). suggestion-weights.dto.ts allows weighted matching. suggest-serial-number.dto.ts returns candidates. The BarrelMatchingService (referenced in tests) matches scanned data against existing barrels considering: lot variations, serial format variations, location proximity. Resolution templates (resolution-template-mapping.dto.ts) define standard resolution patterns. This helps operators quickly resolve device sync errors by suggesting likely correct values.

The bulk-operations directory contains wizard-steps/ subdirectory with step components and an index.ts barrel export. BulkEditBarrelModal and bulk operation pages use these steps. The wizard pattern: step 1 (select barrels), step 2 (configure operation), step 3 (preview/confirm), step 4 (execute/monitor). Each step is a separate component with props for navigation and state. This structure supports different bulk operation types (transfer, ownership, disposition) with shared step logic.

create-from-android-scan.dto.ts defines job creation from device scan. AndroidSyncInbox receives scan data. If no warehouseJobId in scan, system may auto-create job. Job creation considers: eventType, location, operator. EnumJobCreatorSource.ANDROID_DEVICE marks origin. Auto-created jobs start PENDING, transition to IN_PROGRESS on first scan processing. This enables: field-initiated workflows, operator flexibility, reduced planning overhead. Scan context preserved for audit trail.

Timezone handling in BarrelEventService: 1) isEasternDST(year, month, day) determines EDT vs EST, 2) DST rules: starts 2nd Sunday of March 2AM, ends 1st Sunday of November 2AM, 3) getNthSundayOfMonth() calculates specific Sunday day-of-month, 4) For months 0-1, 11 (Jan, Feb, Dec): always EST, 5) For months 3-9 (Apr-Oct): always EDT, 6) For March (month 2): compare day vs secondSunday, 7) For November (month 10): compare day vs firstSunday, 8) Used when creating eventTime: adjusts for -5 (EST) or -4 (EDT) offset, 9) Ensures consistent timestamps regardless of server timezone, 10) Critical for warehouse operations spanning DST transitions.

EnumDeviceSyncErrorStatus tracks error state: RETRY (awaiting retry), RESOLVED (fixed), UNRESOLVED (needs manual intervention), REJECTED (not fixable). EnumDeviceSyncErrorResolutionType documents how resolved: SINGLE_RESOLUTION (individual fix), BULK_RESOLUTION (batch fix), AUTOMATED_RETRY_RESOLUTION (system retry succeeded). ResolutionPath defines resolution approach: ACCEPT (accept as-is), RESOLVE (manual fix applied), ACKNOWLEDGE (noted without fix), REJECT (invalid data). AndroidSyncInboxService tracks timeout patterns and provides getTransactionTimeoutRecords() for specific error recovery. Error context preserved via DeviceSyncError entity.

The AuditTrackInterceptor coordinates state capture. Before handler execution, it queries the database for the entity's current state using the ID from request parameters, storing it as beforeState. The handler executes the update. After handler execution, the result (updated entity) becomes afterState. The interceptor computes changedFields by comparing beforeState and afterState objects, identifying which keys have different values. All this data is passed to AuditService.createAuditLog() with operationType: UPDATE. For complex nested objects, the comparison may flatten to changed field paths. Audit entries include both states as JSON in beforeState and afterState columns, enabling full change reconstruction.

Location routing flow in handleEntryOrTransferInEvent(): 1) Extract locationString from data, 2) isHoldingLocationString() checks: !hasWH OR first char not number, AND dashCount <= 5, 3) If holding location: route to createHoldingLocationEntry() with eventTypeName 'Entry', 4) Holding flow: lookup HoldingLocation by name, normalize serial via lot variation, find/create barrel, clear locationId, set holdingLocationId, disposition='InHolding', 5) If storage location: processStorageLocationEntry() called, 6) Storage flow: parseStorageLocationString() extracts 7 components, lookup StorageLocation, check occupancy, handle displacement if needed, set locationId, disposition='InStorage', 7) Both create BarrelEvent with Entry eventTypeId, 8) TransferIn events use same handler, same Entry eventTypeId.

GroupTransactionService constructor: 1) PrismaService - database operations, transactions; 2) AuditService - creates audit logs for all transaction operations; 3) ReconciliationService - handles discrepancy detection and resolution; 4) LotAuditService - tracks lot-level quantity changes. AuditService in turn depends on: PrismaService, ContextService, PubSubService. ReconciliationService depends on: PrismaService, AuditService (circular but managed), ContextService. This tree shows GroupTransactionService as a high-level coordinator that leverages specialized services for audit, reconciliation, and lot tracking.

extractRelevantLocation(locationString): 1) Splits by '-': parts = locationString.split('-'); 2) Validates length >= 7 (full format expected); 3) Ignores first two pieces (typically org/warehouse identifiers); 4) Returns remaining: parts.slice(2).join('-'). Example: '01-WHK01-01-1-061-A-1' -> '01-1-061-A-1' (floor-tier-position-rick-number). This standardization enables: consistent location parsing regardless of leading identifiers, compatibility with different location string formats from devices, separation of routing (first pieces) from physical location (remaining).

ManhourConfigurationModule manages labor hour configurations - how many man-hours are allocated/expected for different operations. This enables: labor cost tracking, productivity metrics (barrels per man-hour), and scheduling. The StaffScheduleModule likely uses these configurations for workforce planning. Combined with barrel event timestamps, the system can calculate actual vs expected labor efficiency for warehouse operations.

EnumLotStatus tracks lot lifecycle: 1) Active - lot is available for operations (filling, transfers). 2) Closed - lot is complete, no more barrels can be added. 3) OnHold - lot temporarily suspended pending investigation. 4) Depleted - all barrels transferred/dumped. 5) Archived - historical record, no longer operational. Transitions are: Active can become Closed (manual), OnHold (investigation), or Depleted (automatic when bblTotal reaches 0). OnHold can return to Active (resolved). Closed lots cannot receive new barrels but existing barrels can be transferred out. Status affects which operations are permitted on lot's barrels.

Lot variation resolution flow: 1) BarrelEventService receives barrelSN (e.g., '25G29AB.10.001'), 2) Serial is split into parts, potentialLotCode extracted (first two parts), 3) LotVariationService.findLotByNumberOrVariation() called - checks both Lot.lotNumber and LotVariation.variationNumber, 4) If canonical lot found with different lotNumber than input, serial is normalized: canonicalLot.lotNumber + '.' + remaining parts, 5) For bruteforce barrel creation, createBarrelByBruteforce() extracts lot code, calls lotVariationService to find canonical, 6) If no lot found, CRITICAL error 'BruteForceCreationFailed:MissingLot' logged via AndroidSyncErrorLoggerService, 7) Normalized serial used for barrel lookup/creation, ensuring all barrels link to canonical lot ID regardless of which variation was scanned.

PrismaService constructor injects @Optional() PubSubProvider. In onModuleInit(): 1) Logs whether PubSubProvider is available; 2) If available, can publish cache invalidation events; 3) If not, logs warning about local-only invalidation. Pattern intent: when entity changes, PrismaService middleware (if implemented) or services could call pubSubProvider.publish('cacheInvalidation', {entity, id}) to notify other instances. Comment notes: 'Automatic cache invalidation middleware is not available in Prisma v6 - Cache invalidation is handled manually in cached services'. Current implementation relies on cached services handling their own invalidation.

Job completion flow: 1) All scans in InventoryCheckStaging reach READY status, 2) User reviews issues, resolves CRITICAL ones (BAD_QR mapping, etc.), 3) Commit mutation called on WarehouseJob, 4) Service iterates READY staging records, 5) For each: creates BarrelEvent via BarrelEventService, 6) Event creation follows same logic as direct sync: location validation, displacement handling, 7) Staging status updated to COMMITTED, 8) WarehouseJob status set to COMPLETED, endedAt timestamp set, 9) Any staging with unresolved CRITICAL issues blocks commit, 10) Commit wrapped in transaction for atomicity, 11) Stats updated: committedCount, errorCount, 12) WebSocket notification for completion.

createReconciliation does NOT use a single wrapping transaction: 1) Creates ReconciliationRecord (single Prisma create); 2) If barrelReconciliation needed, calls reconcileGroupBarrelCount (separate operation); 3) If that fails, updates ReconciliationRecord with error status (separate update); 4) If requiresApproval, creates ApprovalRequest (separate create); 5) Creates AuditLog (separate create). Design decision: allows partial success - reconciliation record exists even if barrel adjustment fails. Trade-off: potential inconsistency vs resilience. The separate operations each have their own transactions. For atomic behavior, would need prisma.$transaction wrapper around all operations.

In createBarrelWithOwnership(data, ownershipInput, tx): 1) Extracts owners array from ownershipInput; 2) Validates at least one owner: if (!owners || owners.length === 0) throws Error('At least one owner required'); 3) Calculates total: totalPercentage = owners.reduce((sum, o) => sum + o.ownershipPercentage, 0); 4) Validates sum: if Math.abs(totalPercentage - 100) > OWNERSHIP_SUM_TOLERANCE throws Error('must sum to 100'); 5) Validates each positive: for (owner of owners) if percentage <= 0 throws. OWNERSHIP_SUM_TOLERANCE constant handles floating-point precision. Within transaction: creates barrel, then creates BarrelOwnership records via createMany with appropriate ownershipType (SOLE_OWNER if 1, JOINT_OWNER if multiple).

Audit subscription flow: 1) AuditDashboardResolver defines @Subscription with nullable return, 2) Filter function checks variables.filter against payload: if no filter, returns true (all events), 3) Can filter by: entityType, entityId, userId, action type, date range, 4) When audit event created (via AuditLogService), publishes to trigger, 5) Payload is AuditLogConnection with edges and pageInfo, 6) Filter evaluates each incoming event against subscription variables, 7) Only matching events delivered to client, 8) Client receives structured audit log with: id, entityType, entityId, action, userId, timestamp, changes (before/after diff), 9) Used for compliance monitoring, real-time activity feeds.

Issue severity flow: 1) Each issue created with errorLevel: INFO, WARN, ERROR, CRITICAL, 2) requiresManualReview flag determines blocking: true = blocks commit, 3) CRITICAL errors (BAD_QR, NOLABEL, missing lot/location) always requiresManualReview=true, 4) WARN (bruteforce, displacement) typically requiresManualReview=false, 5) INFO (duplicate scan, empty confirmed) always requiresManualReview=false, 6) ERROR (invalid format, unresolvable duplicate) requiresManualReview=true, 7) On commit attempt: query issues where requiresManualReview=true AND not resolved, 8) If any found: commit blocked, return error with issue list, 9) Resolution flow: E&T/admin resolves issue, sets resolution in database.

StagingModule handles inventory check staging (temporary holding during audits), while AndroidSyncInboxModule processes incoming data from mobile devices. They're separate because they serve different workflows: staging is for warehouse operations, android sync is for device data ingestion. Both may create or update barrel records but through different validation paths. DeviceSyncErrorManagementModule further handles error recovery for failed mobile syncs, showing the complexity of reliable mobile integration.

In transferOwnership() with partial percentage: 1) Find current ownership for fromCustomer/barrel; 2) Calculate: percentageToTransfer = input or current.percentage; remainingPercentage = current - transfer; 3) Within transaction: end current ownership (validTo = now); create new ownership for toCustomer with transfer percentage; if remainingPercentage > 0, create new ownership for fromCustomer with remaining; 4) Call createOwnershipTransaction with TRANSFER type; 5) Database trigger syncs deprecated barrel.customerId. Example: 100% sole owner transfers 30% -> creates 70% ownership for original + 30% for new owner. Both become joint owners if neither has 100%.

GroupTransactionService injects all three services. During transaction operations: 1) PrismaService handles primary CRUD; 2) After transaction creation, AuditService.createAuditLog() is called to record the operation with before/after state; 3) ReconciliationService is used when transactions need reconciliation (e.g., barrel count mismatches); 4) LotAuditService tracks lot-level changes when transactions affect lot quantities. In createLotLevelTransfer(), after creating the GroupTransaction, it calls LotAuditService to record quantity changes. The pattern: create entity -> audit log -> reconciliation check -> lot audit, ensuring a complete audit trail across multiple dimensions.

Prior location mismatch flow: 1) validateAndDetectIssues() finds barrel with existing locationId, 2) getLocationDescriptor(barrel.locationId) returns current location string, 3) Compare currentLocation !== staging.locationString, 4) If different: create WARN issue 'InventoryCheck:BarrelHadPriorStorage', 5) Description: 'Barrel currently at {current}, scanned at {scanned}', 6) Context: currentLocation, scannedLocation, barrelId, 7) requiresManualReview=false, 8) On commit: barrel location updated to scanned location, 9) Creates audit trail of location change, 10) Enables reconciliation of expected vs actual locations.

AuditService injects ContextService but based on createAuditLog(), context is passed explicitly via CreateAuditLogDto (userId, sessionId, ipAddress, userAgent, correlationId). ContextService likely provides: 1) Request-scoped context storage (user from JWT, request metadata); 2) getCurrentUserId() for resolvers that don't pass explicitly; 3) Request correlation ID for tracing. The AuditService primarily uses passed parameters but ContextService serves as fallback and for request-scoped operations. This separation allows: explicit context passing for clarity, fallback to request context when convenient, async operations that outlive request scope.

Progress logging flow: 1) DatabaseProgressService provides startBatchProgress(), updateBatchProgress(), completeBatchProgress(), 2) startBatchProgress() creates InventoryProcessingProgress record with sessionId, batchNumber, startedAt, 3) logProcessingEvent() creates InventoryProcessingLog with: sessionId, batchNumber, logLevel (INFO/WARN/ERROR/FATAL), logCategory (processing/checkpoint/error/performance), message, details JSON, correlationId, 4) updateBatchProgress() updates counts: processedCount, successCount, errorCount, skippedCount, checkpoint data, 5) updateBatchResourceMetrics() tracks memory/CPU usage, 6) Queries: filter by sessionId, batchNumber, logLevel, dateRange, 7) Used for debugging, audit trail, performance analysis, 8) correlationId links related events across batches.

When a user authenticates via Azure AD, the AzureAdStrategy validates the token and extracts EntraUserInfo (id, userPrincipalName, givenName, surname, jobTitle, department). The UserRegistrationService.registerOrUpdateUser() first tries to find the user by azureAdId. If found, it updates their info and syncs roles from Azure App Registration. If not found by azureAdId, it searches by username. If that exists, it links the existing user to Azure AD. Otherwise, it creates a new user. Role determination prioritizes Azure App Registration roles, then falls back to jobTitle/department mapping (e.g., 'administrator' -> ['administrator'], 'operator' -> ['operator']). Domain restrictions can be enforced via AZURE_AD_ALLOWED_DOMAINS. Auto-registration can be disabled via AZURE_AD_AUTO_REGISTER_USERS.

The reprocessSingleError() method polls AndroidSyncInbox records every 200ms for up to 10 seconds. Success criteria: 1) Record not found AND no new DeviceSyncErrors created - assumed successful processing that cleaned up, 2) sync === true AND no new DeviceSyncErrors - explicitly successful. Failure criteria: 1) New DeviceSyncErrors created for the temp inbox record - processing failed and logged new error, 2) error field populated - explicit error during processing, 3) Still sync === false after timeout - processing hung. The method queries for errors created with the temp record's deviceName and inboxId to detect silent failures that mark sync=true but still create errors.

In turbo.json, both 'build' and 'dev' tasks depend on 'prisma:generate'. The prisma:generate task has cache: false to always run fresh. The backend package.json defines 'prebuild' script that runs 'prisma generate'. This multi-layer approach ensures: 1) Turbo runs prisma:generate first, 2) Even if running npm scripts directly, prebuild catches it. Without generated Prisma Client, TypeScript would fail on @prisma/client imports since the generated types wouldn't exist.

DecompositionStrategy enum defines job splitting: BY_LOCATION (separate job per warehouse zone), BY_CUSTOMER (per customer's barrels), BY_LOT (per lot), BY_DATE_RANGE (by fill date periods), BY_QUANTITY (fixed batch sizes), CUSTOM (user-defined logic), INHERITED (child uses parent's strategy). Parent jobs decompose into child jobs for: parallel execution, progress tracking, partial completion. Child jobs maintain: parentJobId reference, inherited priority, aggregated progress. This enables: large operation management, progress visibility, failure isolation.

AuditService provides getEntityHistory(entityType, entityId) which queries all audit logs for that entity ordered by timestamp. Each entry contains beforeState and afterState, allowing reconstruction of the entity's complete history. changedFields array identifies which fields changed in each update. The system supports: 1) Point-in-time reconstruction - building state at any timestamp, 2) Change timeline - showing when each field changed and by whom, 3) Rollback analysis - identifying which change introduced a problem. For complex entities with relations, audit logs include relation changes. correlationId links related entity changes from a single operation.

Storage location parsing: 1) parseStorageLocationString() in location-string.util.ts receives string like '01-WHK01-01-1-061-A-1', 2) Split by '-' into 7 parts minimum, 3) Extract: floor (parts[0]), warehouse (parts[1]), floor2 (parts[2]), row (parts[3]), bay (parts[4]), rick (parts[5]), tier (parts[6]), 4) Validate each component against expected formats, 5) rick validated via isValidRickValue(): A, B, C, Hallway, 6) Invalid rick defaults to Hallway with warning, 7) extractRelevantLocation() skips first 2 pieces: '01-1-061-A-1', 8) Used in StorageLocation lookup: Prisma.findFirst with exact field match, 9) Throws BadRequestException on invalid format (< 7 parts).

Frontend uses components/AzureAuth/ for MSAL authentication (MSAL_Provider.tsx, SignInButton.tsx) wrapped in AuthContext. Environment variable NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_MODE switches between 'azure' (full Entra ID) and 'demo' (bypass for testing). The graphql-proxy route handles both by requiring bearer tokens in azure mode or allowing passthrough in demo. This enables local development without Azure configuration while production enforces real authentication.

periodBreakdownEventInclude uses Prisma.validator() for type-safe include definition: includes barrel.barrelOwnerships (latest ownership with customer), barrel.location.warehouse, eventType.name, groupTransaction.transactionType, WarehouseJobs.jobType. This precise include: 1) Avoids over-fetching by selecting specific fields; 2) Gets nested data in single query via Prisma's join optimization; 3) Provides all data needed for categorizeTransaction(); 4) Type is inferred via Prisma.BarrelEventGetPayload. Used in period breakdown queries for finance reports where event details determine transaction categorization.

BAD_QR partial data flow: 1) Detect BAD_QR via regex in barrelSerialNumber, 2) Fetch AndroidSyncInbox record with jsonEntityPayload, 3) If jsonEntityPayload exists: contains partial data from partial scan, 4) Data may include: partial lot code, partial barrel number, location hints, 5) Include in issue context: partialData: inboxRecord.jsonEntityPayload, 6) Used by BarrelMatchingService for suggestions, 7) E&T team sees partial data when resolving issue, 8) Helps narrow down which barrel the BAD_QR might be, 9) Context preserved for audit trail.

Backend AuditDashboardModule provides audit-dashboard.service.ts and audit-dashboard.resolver.ts for audit analytics. Frontend app/dashboard/barrel-audit-table has BarrelAuditTable.tsx and related components for displaying audit data. The architecture separates: audit event storage (AuditModule), audit analytics (AuditDashboardModule), and audit display (frontend). This enables real-time audit dashboards with filtering, aggregation, and drill-down without impacting audit write performance.

CommentsModule (plural) handles comment collections and batch operations (listing, filtering comments across entities), while CommentModule (singular) likely handles individual comment operations. This separation follows domain-driven design where aggregate operations differ from single-entity operations. CommentsModule imports AuthModule via forwardRef for permissions and exports CommentsService for use by other feature modules that need to attach comments to their entities.

chat-history.dto.ts defines conversation storage. EnumChatMessageRole: likely User, Assistant, System. Chat history used by: AI agent for context, support team reference, audit trail. Messages linked to: userId, sessionId, timestamp. History enables: multi-turn conversations, context preservation across sessions, conversation analytics. Agent module (agent-chat.dto.ts, agent-stream-events.dto.ts) handles real-time AI interactions. Chat data may be subject to retention policies for privacy compliance.

update-warehouse-job.dto.ts defines updatable fields: title, description, priority, assignedTo, dueDate, notes. Status updates follow state machine rules. Cannot update: completed jobs (except notes), cancelled jobs. Priority changes may: reorder queue, trigger notifications, update SLA expectations. Assignment changes: notify new assignee, track handoff history. Due date changes: recalculate schedule impact, warn of conflicts. All updates create audit records.

InventoryCheckStaging records barrel scans with InventoryCheckStagingStatus: PENDING (received), PROCESSING (being validated), READY (validation complete), APPROVED (passed review), REJECTED (failed review), COMMITTED (applied to inventory), ERROR (system error). InventoryCheckIssue links issues to staging records. The processor auto-transitions job from PENDING to IN_PROGRESS on first scan. Scans are validated against expected barrel locations. Discrepancies create issues for manual review. Approved records trigger barrel location updates; rejected records are logged but not applied.

mapTransferTypeToGroupTransactionType() in groupTransaction service converts transfer types to group transaction types. TransferTypeMapping interface returns: transactionType (EnumGroupTransactionType), direction (EnumFinancialDirection). Mapping enables: consistent type handling, financial tracking, event creation. TRANSFER_IN maps to EnumGroupTransactionType.TRANSFER_IN with direction IN. TRANSFER_OUT maps similarly with direction OUT. This abstraction isolates transfer logic from transaction recording.

search-result.dto.ts defines unified search result format. Search covers EnumAuditEntityType entities: Barrel, Lot, Customer, Transfer, etc. Results include: entityType, entityId, displayName, matchedField, relevanceScore. Search implementation uses: PostgreSQL full-text search, pattern matching on serial numbers, JSON field queries for customer data. Unified search enables: quick navigation, cross-reference discovery, operator efficiency. Results filtered by user permissions and entity visibility.

The frontend package.json includes @react-three/fiber, @react-three/drei, and three.js for 3D rendering. This enables 3D warehouse floor visualizations showing barrel positions in storage locations. Combined with StorageLocation data (warehouse, floor, tier, rick, position), the frontend can render interactive 3D views for warehouse planning, locating barrels, and visualizing occupancy. DragnDrop components may enable drag operations within this 3D context.

In development/e2e modes (NODE_ENV === 'development' or 'e2e'), DefaultAuthGuard provides flexibility: if no Bearer token is provided, it creates a mock user with id 'mockUserId', username 'mockUser', email 'mock@example.com', roles ['user']. If E2E_TEST_BEARER_TOKEN is configured and matches the provided token, it uses a test user. If a different token is provided, it validates against Azure AD normally. In production, all requests require valid Azure AD authentication - no mock users or bypass tokens are accepted. The @Public() decorator can still be used to mark endpoints that don't require any authentication in either environment.

In createBarrelWithOwnership(): totalPercentage = owners.reduce((sum, o) => sum + o.ownershipPercentage, 0); if (Math.abs(totalPercentage - 100) > OWNERSHIP_SUM_TOLERANCE) throws Error. OWNERSHIP_SUM_TOLERANCE (likely 0.01 or similar small value) handles: IEEE 754 floating-point representation issues (e.g., 33.33 + 33.33 + 33.34 might not equal exactly 100), user input rounding, JSON serialization precision loss. This allows percentages like 33.333... to work correctly while still catching obvious errors like sum of 95% or 105%.

EnumBulkOperationStatus tracks multi-record operations: PENDING (queued), RUNNING (executing), COMPLETED (all succeeded), FAILED (all failed), CANCELLED (user cancelled), PARTIAL_SUCCESS (some succeeded). BulkOperation records: operationType, totalRecords, processedRecords, failedRecords, successRecords, errorSummary, executedBy, correlationId. Used by bulk ownership transfers, inventory updates, reconciliation batches. Enables: progress tracking, partial rollback, error reporting per record, resume capability. BulkOwnershipOperationResponse returns individual success/error arrays.

The AuditService uses several strategies for performance: 1) Async logging - createAuditLog() returns immediately after queueing the write, 2) Batch inserts for bulk operations using createAuditLogBatch(), 3) Indexed queries using correlationId, entityId, userId for fast lookups, 4) Pagination for large result sets, 5) The service can be configured to log asynchronously via PubSub, decoupling the write from the request. For extremely high volume, audit logs can be written to a separate database or time-series store. The changedFields array is pre-computed rather than storing full before/after for small changes, reducing storage.

ReconciliationService.createReconciliation() calculates impactLevel using calculateImpactLevel(discrepancies). Each DiscrepancyDetail has severity: 'HIGH', 'MEDIUM', or 'LOW'. The impact level (EnumImpactLevel: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, CRITICAL) is determined by: 1) Number of discrepancies. 2) Highest severity among discrepancies. 3) Affected entity type sensitivity. For GroupTransaction or OwnershipTransactionGroup entities with barrel reconciliation, the service can automatically apply barrel adjustments (add/remove barrels). Failed barrel reconciliation updates status to PENDING with error context but doesn't fail the entire reconciliation creation.

The UserRegistrationService.validateUserRegistration() checks AZURE_AD_ALLOWED_DOMAINS environment variable. If configured, it splits the comma-separated list into allowed domains. It extracts the user's domain from userPrincipalName (part after @). If the user's domain is not in the allowed list, registration is rejected with reason 'Domain {domain} is not allowed'. This prevents users from unauthorized Azure AD tenants from accessing the system even if they have valid Azure AD credentials. Additionally, AZURE_AD_AUTO_REGISTER_USERS can be set to false to completely disable automatic user creation, requiring manual user setup.

Long-running mutations use several patterns: 1) TimeoutInterceptor with @Timeout() decorator sets per-operation timeouts (e.g., 30s for resolutions), 2) Prisma transactions have their own timeout option, 3) For very long operations, the mutation returns immediately with a job ID, and clients poll for status or subscribe to completion events, 4) Background processing uses BullMQ queues. The GraphQL context passes through to async operations via ContextService. For bulk operations, progress can be reported via subscriptions. Error handling ensures partial failures are properly rolled back or recorded for manual intervention.

PurchaseOrderModule handles incoming orders (buying barrels/spirits), SalesOrderModule handles outgoing orders (selling inventory). Both integrate with CustomerModule for customer data and TransferModule for inventory movement. The architecture supports: purchase order receipt (barrels in), sales order fulfillment (barrels out), and the complete order lifecycle from creation to completion. Financial transactions link to these orders for accounting integration.

calculateNewLotNumber(currentLotNumber, input): 1) If input.newLotNumber provided, returns it directly (explicit override); 2) If input.newLotSuffix provided, returns currentLotNumber + suffix (e.g., '23A.123' + '-B' = '23A.123-B'); 3) Otherwise returns currentLotNumber unchanged. Used in executeBarrelTransfer() to determine if updateBarrelLotNumber() needs to be called. This enables: ownership transfers with lot reassignment, lot splitting scenarios with suffix, preserving lot when just changing owner. The suffix pattern supports customer-specific lot variations.

The createMockClient() function in the frontend client creates a fully functional mock LDClient. It maintains internal state: mockContext for user context, mockFlags Record for cached flag values, and eventListeners Map for change subscriptions. The variation() method checks for dev overrides first, then returns the default value, caching results in mockFlags. identify() updates mockContext and emits 'change' event to trigger subscribed components to refresh. on()/off() manage event listeners. allFlags() returns the cached mockFlags. This allows development without a LaunchDarkly account while maintaining the same API contract. The mock is automatically used when LAUNCHDARKLY_CLIENT_ID is not set.

Exclusive constraint handling: 1) PostgreSQL check constraint on Barrel table: locationId XOR holdingLocationId (not both), 2) When moving barrel to storage: must clear holdingLocationId before setting locationId, 3) In createHoldingLocationEntry(): update barrel with locationId=null, holdingLocationId=holdingLocation.id, 4) In storage entry: clear holdingLocationId, set locationId, 5) Constraint violation throws database error, 6) Prevents barrel being in two places simultaneously, 7) All updates within transaction for atomicity, 8) disposition field tracks: InStorage, InHolding, Withdrawn, etc.

Staging state machine: 1) Initial state: record created in InventoryCheckStaging with status PROCESSING, processingStartedAt set, 2) States: PROCESSING (validation in progress), READY (validation complete, awaiting review), APPROVED (human approved), COMMITTED (changes applied), REJECTED, 3) Transitions: PROCESSING->READY on successful validation, 4) hasIssues flag set based on detected issues, 5) WarehouseJob stats updated: totalScans, pendingReview, issueCount, criticalIssueCount, 6) Job auto-transitions PENDING->IN_PROGRESS on first scan, 7) Stuck detection via processingStartedAt, lastHeartbeat, processingAttempts, 8) Commit flow: READY->APPROVED->COMMITTED, creates actual BarrelEvents, 9) Issues with requiresManualReview=true block approval.

BarrelOwnershipService imports createId from @paralleldrive/cuid2. Used in: 1) createOwnershipTransaction() - generates unique transaction ID; 2) establishJointOwnership() - generates jointOwnershipGroupId; 3) bulkUpdateOwnership() - generates operationId; 4) executeBarrelTransfer() - generates ownership record ID. CUID2 provides: collision-resistant IDs, sortable by creation time, URL-safe characters, suitable for distributed systems without coordination. This replaces UUID in some cases for better sorting/indexing characteristics.

ResolutionValidationUtil provides several message generation methods: createExtractedFieldsErrorMessage() explains which fields couldn't be auto-extracted and why they're needed for the resolution option. createMissingFieldsErrorMessage() lists required fields that weren't provided by the user, with context about what each field represents. Both methods include errorType and resolutionOption in the message for context. getRequiredFields() returns the list of fields required for a specific error type and resolution option combination. This enables the frontend to show targeted validation errors and the API to return actionable error responses that guide users to provide correct data.

Transaction handling: 1) $androidSyncTransaction is Prisma interactive transaction with SERIALIZABLE isolation, 2) Provides transactionClient to callback function, 3) SERIALIZABLE prevents dirty reads, non-repeatable reads, phantom reads, 4) Deadlock scenario: two transactions lock same rows in different order, 5) PostgreSQL detects deadlock, aborts one transaction with error, 6) Caller catches error, can retry with exponential backoff, 7) TEC-1173 FIX: when tx provided externally, use it (avoid nested transaction), 8) Pattern: executeWithTransaction accepts any client, caller decides isolation, 9) Long transactions avoided by using CachedEventTypeService for reference data.

GroupTransactionModule handles general transaction grouping (batching operations), OwnershipTransactionModule tracks individual ownership changes (transfers, inheritances), and OwnershipTransactionGroupModule groups ownership changes into atomic operations. For example, a bulk ownership transfer creates an OwnershipTransactionGroup containing multiple OwnershipTransactions. GroupTransaction may wrap both ownership and other transaction types. This hierarchy enables both granular audit trails and atomic batch operations.

Chain: 1) InventoryUploadService creates session, validates file; 2) InventoryProcessingProcessor dequeues job; 3) InventoryProcessingService parses CSV rows; 4) For each row, calls barrel creation logic which uses BarrelService.createBarrelWithOwnership(); 5) BarrelService validates data, creates barrel, creates BarrelOwnership records; 6) Within transaction, may call LotVariationService for lot resolution; 7) AuditService logs the operations; 8) ProgressTrackingService updates upload progress; 9) SessionManagementService manages session state. Each service has distinct responsibility: parsing, validation, persistence, audit, progress tracking.

ThrottlerStorageRedisProvider creates a Redis-backed storage for the ThrottlerModule. Instead of each server instance maintaining its own in-memory counter (which would allow rate*instances requests), all instances share state in Redis. The storage key includes the client identifier (typically IP) and throttle tier. Redis atomic operations (INCR, EXPIRE) ensure accurate counting even with concurrent requests across instances. In development/test, the provider returns undefined to use default in-memory storage. This is configured in AppModule's ThrottlerModule.forRootAsync() using the provider's getStorage() method.

The @Public() decorator uses applyDecorators to combine two metadata settings. PublicAuthMiddleware sets IS_PUBLIC_KEY to true, which auth guards check via Reflector to bypass authentication. PublicAuthSwagger sets 'swagger/apiSecurity' metadata to ['isPublic'], which Swagger/OpenAPI generators use to mark the endpoint as not requiring authentication in API documentation. This dual approach ensures: 1) Runtime auth bypass works correctly, 2) Generated API docs accurately reflect that the endpoint is publicly accessible. The decorator can be applied at controller or method level, with method-level taking precedence for nested configurations.

BarrelAggregates module provides: barrel-oem-count.dto.ts (count by cooperage), barrel-period-count.dto.ts (count by time period), barrel-period-oem-count.dto.ts (combined), barrel-recipe-count.dto.ts (count by recipe), barrel-size-count.dto.ts (count by barrel size). inventory-aggregates.dto.ts combines all dimensions. location-aggregate.dto.ts provides warehouse utilization. inventory-by-recipe.dto.ts supports blend planning. These aggregations enable: dashboard KPIs, capacity planning, customer reporting, production scheduling without querying every barrel.

The data flow is: 1) Android device sends scan data to AndroidSyncInbox via REST API, 2) The android-sync.processor.ts picks up the inbox record from the BullMQ queue, 3) The processor fetches the AndroidSyncInbox record using the deviceName and inboxId compound key, 4) BarrelEventService.createBarrelEventWithLocationAndLocationCheck() is called which routes to appropriate handler based on eventType (Entry, InventoryCheck, Relocation, etc.), 5) The service uses Prisma.$androidSyncTransaction for serializable isolation to prevent race conditions, 6) Barrel is looked up/created via findOrCreateBarrel with lot variation normalization, 7) Location is resolved (storage or holding), 8) BarrelEvent is created and barrel.locationId/holdingLocationId is updated, 9) WebSocket progress updates are broadcast via ProgressGateway.

The components/email-workflow directory handles email parsing and processing workflows for incoming transfer requests or customer communications. Combined with useAIEmailParser and useEmailParser hooks, plus app/api/parse-email-ai/route.ts, this supports extracting structured data from emails using AI. The architecture enables: receiving emails -> AI parsing -> structured data extraction -> workflow initiation, reducing manual data entry for warehouse operations.

validateUserId(userId, tx?): 1) If !userId returns undefined; 2) If userId === 'system', calls ensureSystemUser(tx) which finds or creates the system user; 3) Otherwise queries user.findUnique({ where: { id: userId } }); 4) If user not found, logs warning and falls back to ensureSystemUser(tx). ensureSystemUser(): tries to find user with username='system', if not found creates with minimal data (password='system', roles={system:true}). This enables: audit logs always having valid userId FK, graceful handling of deleted users, support for system-initiated operations. The tx parameter allows this to work within existing transactions.

The HttpExceptionsFilter catches all exceptions and formats them consistently. It extracts the HTTP status from HttpException or defaults to 500 for unknown errors. The response includes statusCode, message (from exception.getResponse()), timestamp in ISO format, and path from request.url. For GraphQL contexts, it uses GqlArgumentsHost to extract the request. The filter logs errors with stack traces for debugging. It handles Prisma errors by mapping constraint violations to 400 Bad Request. The business-logic-error-handler.ts provides additional error categorization (RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, etc.) before errors reach the filter. Custom exceptions like BadRequestException include enhanced context in their message.

The components/filters directory contains ResponsiveFilterBar/ with index.ts (barrel file for exports) and useActiveFilters.ts (hook for filter state). FilterPersistenceBridge.tsx connects to URL state for shareable filter links. FilterPresets.tsx allows saving/loading filter configurations. This architecture separates filter UI rendering (responsive breakpoints), state management (active filters hook), persistence (URL/local storage), and presets (saved configurations) into composable pieces.

WhiskeyTypeModule manages whiskey type classifications (bourbon, rye, corn, etc.). This categorical data links to recipes and lots for product identification. The module provides: type definitions, validation, and querying. Combined with Recipe and Lot modules, it creates a hierarchy: WhiskeyType -> Recipe -> Lot -> Barrel. This enables inventory reporting by spirit type and ensures products are correctly categorized for regulatory compliance.

The resolution system supports creating or linking subsequent jobs via ResolutionFunctionParams.subsequentJob. If existingJobId is provided, the resolution links to that existing job. If newJobData is provided, it creates a new warehouse job with the specified metadata: description, priority, status, createdBy (required email), dueDate, eventType, jobType (required), instructions, estimatedDuration, and notes. The assignedUserNames array allows assigning users by name/email. After resolution execution, if subsequentJobId is returned, the DeviceSyncResolution record is updated to connect to the subsequentJobs relation. This enables workflows where resolving an error triggers follow-up inventory checks or manual verification tasks.

Throughout BarrelOwnershipService methods (createOwnership, transferOwnership, establishJointOwnership, etc.), there are comments: 'Note: Deprecated barrel customer fields are automatically synced by DB trigger'. This indicates: 1) Barrel table had legacy customerId/customerCode fields; 2) New design uses BarrelOwnership table for ownership; 3) Database triggers sync the new ownership to deprecated fields for backward compatibility. The service intentionally does NOT update barrel.customerId directly - it only creates/updates BarrelOwnership records. The trigger (defined in migrations) automatically keeps Barrel.customerId in sync with the most recent active BarrelOwnership. This enables gradual migration without breaking legacy code.

The resolution system uses database transactions with Prisma to handle concurrency. Within the transaction, it verifies errors are unresolved before proceeding. If two requests try to resolve the same error simultaneously, one will succeed and mark the error as RESOLVED. The second request's check for 'already resolved' errors will find the error already resolved and throw BadRequestException. The transaction's serializable isolation ensures consistent reads. Additionally, the verification step after resolution updates catches race conditions where the status update might have failed silently. This optimistic concurrency control prevents double-resolution without requiring explicit locking.

In validateBarrelCreationData(): 1) Extracts hasStorageLocation by checking if location.connect.id OR locationId is set; 2) Extracts hasHoldingLocation by checking holdingLocation.connect.id OR holdingLocationId; 3) If BOTH are true, throws BarrelLocationConflictException. Additionally validates disposition compatibility via validateDispositionLocationState(): storage location requires 'InStorage' disposition; holding location requires 'InHolding' or 'TransferredOffsite'; no location forbids 'InStorage' or 'InHolding'. Database has constraint chk_barrel_location_exclusive as backup. This multi-layer validation ensures business rule enforcement at service and database levels.

The DefaultAuthGuard explicitly handles OPTIONS requests for CORS preflight support. Before any authentication logic, it checks if request.method === 'OPTIONS'. If so, it returns true immediately, bypassing all authentication. This is necessary because CORS preflight requests are browser-generated checks that never carry credentials. Without this bypass, cross-origin requests would fail at the preflight stage even for public endpoints. The guard logs 'OPTIONS request detected, skipping authentication for CORS preflight' for debugging. The actual CORS headers are set by the server's CORS configuration, not the auth guard. This pattern is documented with TEC-1036 reference.

Rate limiter configuration: 1) @Processor decorator includes limiter: { max: 10, duration: 1000 }, 2) Limits to 10 jobs per second (1000ms window), 3) Prevents queue bloat under burst traffic (many scans at once), 4) Jobs exceeding limit queued and processed at limited rate, 5) Combined with concurrency: 5 - up to 5 parallel workers, 6) Effective throughput: 10 jobs/sec start, 5 concurrent execution, 7) Protects database from overwhelming concurrent writes, 8) Prevents Redis memory bloat from queued jobs, 9) Tunable based on infrastructure capacity.

The populate_barrel_details() SQL function ensures lot exists before barrel creation: Step 5 first searches Lot table by lotNumber, creates new lot if not found (with createdBy, updatedBy from p_entry_by parameter). Only after v_lot_id is obtained does Step 7 create/update barrel with lotId. If lot handling fails, v_error_message is set and barrel creation is skipped. The Barrel.lotId foreign key references Lot.id, and Prisma enforces referential integrity. This prevents orphaned barrels and ensures complete lot-barrel relationships for inventory tracking.

transformLotForMES() transforms WMS Lot to MES format: 1) Converts WhiskeyType relation to MES enum via mapWhiskeyTypeCodeToEnum(lot.WhiskeyType?.code); 2) Maps relation globalIds: recipeGlobalId, productionOrderGlobalId, furnishingCustomerGlobalId from nested relations; 3) Includes core fields: id, globalId, lotNumber, externalId, schemaVersion; 4) Includes metrics: bblTotal, totalPGs, totalWGs, etc.; 5) Excludes WMS-specific fields not in MES schema. This enables: bidirectional sync with MES, schema versioning via schemaVersion field, maintaining data consistency across systems. The transformation is defensive with null checks.

turbo.json's globalEnv (NODE_ENV, CI, DATABASE_URL, DB_URL) and globalPassThroughEnv (VERCEL, VERCEL_ENV) define environment variables that affect all tasks' cache keys. Changes to these variables invalidate all caches, triggering full rebuilds. Task-specific env lists (in 'build', 'dev', 'start') add additional variables per task. This configuration ensures builds are reproducible: same env vars produce same outputs, but env changes correctly bust caches.

TransferService.getTransfersForReconciliation() calculates reconciliation status by comparing expected vs actual barrel counts: 1) currentBarrelCount = groupTransaction.affectedBarrelIds.length (actual barrels linked). 2) expectedBarrelCount = transfer.quantity (expected from transfer record). 3) needsReconciliation = currentBarrelCount === 0 && expectedBarrelCount > 0. Statuses are: NOT_RECONCILED (0 barrels), PARTIALLY_RECONCILED (barrels < expected), FULLY_RECONCILED (barrels >= expected). The DB query filters transfers with quantity OR group transactions to exclude bulk/gallon-based transfers that don't involve individual barrel tracking.

Circuit breaker reset: 1) After batch processing, if batchResult.errorCount === 0, 2) Reset metrics.consecutiveErrors = 0, 3) Call safetyLimitsService.resetCircuitBreaker(sessionId), 4) Service clears any error state for session, 5) Allows processing to continue without accumulated penalty, 6) If errors: increment consecutiveErrors, no reset, 7) Circuit breaker pattern: prevents cascading failures, 8) After MAX_CONSECUTIVE_ERRORS: circuit open, processing halts, 9) Recovery: manual intervention or time-based auto-reset.

DataValidationService orchestrates three validation phases: 1) validateStorageLocations() - calls PostgreSQL function validate_storage_locations() to verify location strings map to valid StorageLocation records. 2) validateBusinessEntities() - calls validate_business_entities_with_auto_lot_creation() to validate lots, customers, barrels with optional auto-creation. 3) checkDataIntegrity() - calls validate_duplicate_barrels() to detect duplicates. Each phase returns typed result (StorageLocationValidationResult, BusinessEntityValidationResult, DataIntegrityValidationResult) with success flag, counts, errors, duration, and phase-specific statistics. Validation can be skipped via ValidationOptions flags.

The client uses a singleton pattern with initializationPromise to prevent race conditions. If initializeFeatureFlagClient() is called while initialization is in progress, it returns the existing promise rather than starting a new initialization. Multiple components calling initialize concurrently all await the same promise. The ldClient variable is only set after successful initialization. getFeatureFlag() calls initialize internally, ensuring the client is ready before evaluation. This prevents: multiple LaunchDarkly connections, inconsistent flag states during initialization, and wasted API calls. The pattern is thread-safe for concurrent React component mounting.

AndroidSyncProcessor depends on: CachedEventTypeService, CachedEventReasonService, CachedLotVariationService, CachedStorageLocationService. Cache warming in onModuleInit() calls warmAllCaches() which uses Promise.all() to parallelize: cachedEventTypeService.warmCache(), cachedEventReasonService.warmCache(), cachedLotVariationService.warmCache(100), cachedStorageLocationService.warmCache('WHK01'). This is done because: 1) BullMQ processes jobs with high concurrency (2 concurrent jobs); 2) Without pre-warming, the first job would trigger cache-miss database queries causing latency; 3) Reference data (event types, reasons, locations) is frequently accessed but rarely changes, making it ideal for caching; 4) Pre-warming ensures consistent low-latency processing regardless of which job runs first.

Connection stats: 1) ProgressGateway.getConnectionStats() returns metrics, 2) totalConnections: activeConnections.size (Map of authenticated sockets), 3) totalSessionSubscriptions: sessionSubscriptions.size (Map of sessions), 4) connectionsPerSession: array mapping sessionId to subscriber count, 5) Built from sessionSubscriptions entries, 6) Used for: monitoring, debugging, capacity planning, 7) Can expose via admin endpoint or health check, 8) Track subscriber growth, identify popular sessions.

get-aggregated-staging-records.dto.ts provides staging summary for warehouse jobs. process-barrel-fill-job.dto.ts, process-bottling-job.dto.ts, process-tanker-load-job.dto.ts define job-specific processing. Aggregation includes: total records, by status counts, issue breakdown. Job status progresses based on staging completion. SCANNING_COMPLETE status requires: all expected scans received, staging records processed, issues flagged. Aggregation enables: progress tracking, bottleneck identification, job completion estimation.

The audit module design supports Prisma middleware integration for automatic audit logging. The AuditService includes middleware-aware interfaces like PrismaMiddlewareParams defining model, action, and args. For Prisma v6+ (which removed $use middleware), the CacheInvalidationMiddleware shows the reference pattern. Services must now call AuditService directly after mutations. The middleware would have intercepted all write operations (create, update, delete) and called AuditService.createAuditLog() automatically. Current implementation requires explicit @AuditTrack() decorator or manual service calls. The beforeState is captured pre-mutation, afterState from mutation result, enabling automatic before/after diff computation.

Duplicate scan detection: 1) In validateAndDetectIssues() Phase 3, 2) findDuplicateScans(warehouseJobId, barrelSerialNumber, currentStagingId), 3) Queries InventoryCheckStaging: count where jobId matches AND serialNumber matches AND id !== current, 4) If duplicateCount > 0: create INFO issue 'DuplicateScan', 5) Description: 'Barrel {SN} scanned {count+1} times in this job', 6) Context: duplicateCount (total including current), 7) requiresManualReview=false, resolutionAction='IGNORE', 8) On commit: only one scan processed, others ignored, 9) Handles operator re-scans, ensures barrel not double-counted.

Complete flow: 1) InventoryUploadService.createUploadSession() creates session; 2) Controller saves file, adds job to 'inventory-processing' queue; 3) InventoryProcessingProcessor dequeues batch job; 4) MicroBatchOrchestratorService manages batch size/pacing; 5) For each CSV row: SchemaValidationService validates; BarrelProcessingService transforms data; 6) BarrelService.createBarrelWithOwnership() or createBarrelWithSoleOwnership() called; 7) Within transaction: validates data, creates Barrel, creates BarrelOwnership records; 8) LotVariationService resolves lot if needed; 9) AuditService logs barrel creation; 10) ProgressTrackingService updates session progress; 11) SessionPhaseTrackingService tracks phase completion; 12) On complete, InventoryUploadService.updateSessionStatus() finalizes. 12+ services coordinated per barrel.

upload-csv.dto.ts defines CSV field mapping. validation.dto.ts defines validation result types. DataValidationService.validateSchemaCompliance() uses Zod schema validation on staging rows. CSV rows mapped to: Notes, Customer, Warehouse, Floor, Tier, Rick, Position, Lot#, Mashbill, Barrel#. Validation phases: 1) Schema compliance (field types/formats). 2) Storage location existence. 3) Business entity validity (lots, customers). 4) Duplicate detection. Invalid rows marked with error details; valid rows proceed to processing.

EnumConfigSyncType, EnumConfigSyncAction, EnumConfigSyncStatus manage configuration synchronization. printer-config-sync.dto.ts handles printer settings distribution. EnumLogoSyncStatus, EnumLogoSyncAction, EnumLogoSyncRequestStatus manage customer logo distribution. Full sync (EnumFullSyncStatus) handles complete configuration refresh. This enables: consistent settings across Android devices, centralized logo management, version-controlled configuration. Sync failures create retry records for eventual consistency.

Table-new contains reusable table components: BarrelInventoryTable.tsx, BarrelReceiptsTable.tsx (feature-specific tables), SortableColumnHeader.tsx, Pagination.tsx (shared components), and Views/ subdirectory for different view modes. The 'new' suffix suggests it replaced an older implementation. This structure allows feature-specific tables to compose shared components while each table defines its columns, filters, and data sources. FilterStatusDisplay.tsx and FilterPresets.tsx add filter capabilities.

The ComplianceReportingService generates reports by querying AuditLog records with various filters. It supports date range filtering, entity type filtering, operation type filtering, and user filtering. Reports include summary statistics (total operations, success/failure counts), detailed operation logs with before/after states, and user activity summaries. The AuditIntegrityValidatorService validates report integrity using hash chains - each audit log entry's hash includes the previous entry's hash, creating a tamper-evident chain. Reports can be exported in various formats for regulatory submission. The system tracks sensitive operations like DELETE, role changes, and security-related actions with enhanced detail.
